1.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
1.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

2.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

4.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

5.gl: a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
5.kj: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
